decisions.candidate_summary: Add a standing compliance-first analytical frame for AI vendor procurement decisions in regulated environments, covering data residency, BAA coverage, certifications (SOC2/FedRAMP/HIPAA), audit trails, and shadow-IT exposure.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Signal strength 0.82 is genuine - first appearance, no stuck-candidate inflation. The underlying concern (AI tooling compliance in regulated environments) is real and relevant. However, the source post (HN/47043484) is the same one already cited in c5-prof-004, indicating this is re-processed signal, not new signal.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Direct duplicate of existing configuration. c5-prof-004 (2026-05-23) already establishes compliance-first evaluation methodology for AI tools with BAA, FedRAMP, and data routing as leading findings. Skill install ee870847 (2026-06-02) elevates these to blocking disqualifiers. c6-prof-001 (2026-05-28) adds proactive surfacing of shadow-IT and ungoverned tooling exposure. The candidate proposes what already exists across three entries. Only SOC2/HIPAA certification checks would be marginally additive.
reasoning.eval_alignment: Empty eval_history prevents outcome-based inference. Change_log shows this category of change has been applied three times across Cycles 5-7, suggesting user values it but also suggesting the pipeline has already fully acted on this signal class.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Low risk in either direction. Applying creates configuration bloat and maintenance burden across four semantically overlapping entries. Skipping preserves clean configuration given existing coverage. The candidate is not stuck (prior_appearances: 0) - this is pipeline re-processing of already-acted-upon signal, not an ignored candidate resurfacing.

hypothesis.prediction: Skipping this candidate will produce no degradation in AI vendor compliance evaluation behavior because the proposed functionality already exists across c5-prof-004, skill install ee870847, and c6-prof-001.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: A user interaction reveals a gap in AI vendor compliance evaluation that this candidate would have addressed but the existing three entries did not.

decision_record.session_notes: Single-candidate batch representing pipeline noise. The digest generated a candidate from HN/47043484 that has already been processed and acted upon in Cycle 5 (c5-prof-004) using the same source. This suggests the semantic deduplication layer is not catching cross-cycle duplicates when phrasing differs. The fingerprint mechanism appears to be producing new fingerprints for semantically identical proposals. Recommend: add change_log source-URL matching to the assembler's suppression logic to catch candidates derived from already-processed source posts.
